name: Willard Scott's Pound Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together butter and shortening in a large bowl until smooth.
2. Add eggs one at a time, creaming after each addition.
3. Gradually add sugar to the mixture, continuing to beat until well combined.
4. Sift flour and baking powder into a separate bowl.
5. Alternately add the sifted dry ingredients with milk to the butter mixture, beating on low speed after each addition.
6. Pour batter into a greased and floured 10-inch bundt pan.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 325°F for 1-1/4 to 1-1/2 hours until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Frosting:
1. Toast pecans lightly in a broiler pan with softened butter until well browned, then let cool slightly.
2. In a bowl, combine chopped pecans, powdered sugar, and enough milk to reach a spreading consistency.
3. Spread frosting evenly over the top of the cake, allowing some to drip down the sides and center.
